query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Volatile', 'Likely to change suddenly and unpredictably', 'Over time, the community learned to volatile its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Volition', 'A conscious choice or decision', 'The essay highlights the volition of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Voluble', 'Characterised by a ready and continuous flow of words', 'Scholars noted a striking voluble between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Voluminous', 'Of great size or extent', 'The passage presents a voluminous portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Voluptuous', 'Suggestive of or characterized by full, generous sensation', 'What seemed voluptuous at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Voracious', 'Having a great appetite', '“He was a voracious reader.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vortex', 'A whirlwind, moving matter in the form of a spiral', 'Scholars noted a striking vortex between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Votary', 'A person bound by solemn religious vows, as a monk or nun', 'Scholars noted a striking votary between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Votive', 'Dedicated or given in fulfillment of a vow or pledge', 'The reviewer described the tone as votive, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vouchsafe', 'To grant or give, as by favor, graciousness or condescension', 'Critics argued that the plan might vouchsafe the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vulgarity', 'The quality of being crude or tasteless', 'Scholars noted a striking vulgarity between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Vulnerable', 'Capable of or susceptible to being hurt or wounded', 'Over time, the community learned to vulnerable its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Waft', 'To carry lightly and smoothly through the air or over water', 'Critics argued that the plan might waft the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Waggish', 'Roguish in merriment and good humor', 'Her waggish became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Waif', 'A person, especially a child, who has no home or friends', 'Scholars noted a striking waif between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Waive', 'To refrain from claiming or insisting on', 'The author hoped the new policy would waive tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Wallow', 'To move lazily or heavily', 'The author hoped the new policy would wallow tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Wampum', 'Formerly native american peoples'' currency', 'Scholars noted a striking wampum between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Wane', 'To decrease in size or intensity', 'The author hoped the new policy would wane tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Wanton', 'Deliberate and unprovoked; reckless', 'Scholars noted a striking wanton between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Wantonness', 'The state of being reckless', 'The essay highlights the wantonness of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Warily', 'In a careful manner', 'She spoke warily, leaving little doubt about her position.', 'adverb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Warlike', 'Hostile and belligerent', 'Scholars noted a striking warlike between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Warp', 'To bend or twist out of shape', 'The author hoped the new policy would warp tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Warrant', 'To justify or guarantee', '“The situation warrants immediate action.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Wary', 'Feeling caution about possible danger', 'Her wary became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Wastrel', 'One who wastes time or resources extravagantly', 'Scholars noted a striking wastrel between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Wavelet', 'A small wave, ripple', 'Scholars noted a striking wavelet between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Waver', 'To sway back and forth, to totter or reel', 'Critics argued that the plan might waver the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Waylay', 'To lie in wait for and attack from ambush', 'Critics argued that the plan might waylay the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Weal', 'Well-being, prosperity or happiness', 'Scholars noted a striking weal between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Wean', 'To accustom a child to food other than mother''s milk', 'Critics argued that the plan might wean the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Wearisome', 'Tiresome, tedious or causing fatigue', 'The essay highlights the wearisome of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Wee', 'Little, very small', 'The essay highlights the wee of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Weighty', 'Having weight, heavy, ponderous, as, a weighty body', 'The passage presents a weighty portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Welter', 'To roll, toss or heave', 'Critics argued that the plan might welter the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Wheedle', 'To influence a person by smooth, flattering words or acts', 'The author hoped the new policy would wheedle tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Whelp', 'To give birth', 'Critics argued that the plan might whelp the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Wherewith', 'With which', 'The essay highlights the wherewith of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Whet', 'To sharpen by grinding or friction', 'The author hoped the new policy would whet tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Whim', 'A fanciful impulse, or whimsical idea', 'Her whim became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Whimsical', 'Playfully quaint or fanciful', 'The passage presents a whimsical portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Whine', 'To utter a complaining cry or sound', 'Critics argued that the plan might whine the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Wholly', 'Entirely', 'She spoke wholly, leaving little doubt about her position.', 'adverb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Wield', 'To command, rule over, to possess or own', 'The author hoped the new policy would wield tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Wile', 'A trick, artifice or stratagem meant to fool, trap or entice', 'Over time, the community learned to wile its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Willful', 'Deliberate, voluntary or intentional', 'The passage presents a willful portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Wily', 'Sly, cunning, full of tricks', 'The results were wily linked to changes in public policy.', 'adverb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Winnow', 'To remove lighter particles from grain', 'The author hoped the new policy would winnow tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Winsome', 'Attractive or appealing in appearance or character', 'What seemed winsome at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
